sentences.input: Paul Michael Corballis is a New Zealand cognitive neuroscientist and professor of Psychology at the University of Auckland.

sentences.input: His research focuses on visual perception and attention, and uses electrophysiology (EEG/ERPs) and neuroimaging methods.
Corballis is the son of academic psychologist Michael Corballis.

sentences.fol_kripke: Exists x((BSc(x, w0)  And  Exists e(Complete(e, w0)  And  Agent(e, Him, w0)  And  Theme(e, x, w0)  And  Past(e, w0))))
sentences.input: He completed a BSc in Psychology (1989) and an MSc in Psychology with First Class Honours (1991) at the University of Auckland.

sentences.fol_kripke: Exists x((PhD(x, w0)  And  Exists e(Earn(e, w0)  And  Agent(e, Him, w0)  And  Theme(e, x, w0)  And  Past(e, w0))))
sentences.input: He earned a PhD in Psychology from Columbia University in 1997.
After his doctorate, Corballis held a postdoctoral position in the Department of Psychological and Brain Sciences at Dartmouth College.

sentences.input: He joined the faculty at the University of Auckland School of Psychology, where he is a professor of Psychology.

sentences.error: I was looking for a noun, verb, or adjective at position 3, but found Preposition(Symbol(1)) instead. The logic needs a content word to ground it.
sentences.input: In an inaugural lecture hosted by the Faculty of Science, he discussed "brain mechanisms of constructive perception."

sentences.fol_kripke: Gen x((Corballis(x, w0)  Implies  Study(x, Mechanisms, w0)))
sentences.input: Corballis studies the neural mechanisms that support selective attention and visual perception, often using event-related potentials such as the N2pc, together with other EEG and neuroimaging techniques.
Early in his career he co-authored work demonstrating noninvasive optical imaging of human brain responses during visual stimulation.
His later publications and collaborations include work on lateralized ERP components associated with attentional selection.
